body: WARRANT#81 shipped -> SLS(86)/10.00. Continued forge-indicate's r5: WARRANT#42 had only built the hysteresis half of threshold-to-target, leaving the win-rate-target-driven threshold SETTING deferred -- meanwhile WARRANT#68 had already formalized a real reliability curve (predicted-probability decile -> realized WR) that nothing ever queried. Added RI_ThresholdForTargetWR() to answer that directly: given a WR target, which predicted-probability decile actually clears it, per the already-measured curve. Diagnostic only, surfaced in the existing reliability-curve report, not wired into any live threshold. Self-caught and fixed a process slip mid-pass: first edit landed in-place on the already-deployed SLS(85) file instead of copying to SLS(86) first -- restored SLS(85) byte-identical from the terminal's deployed copy before redoing it correctly. Compiled 0 errors, diff-verified purely additive, deployed to the terminal's real Experts folder, ledger at pass_66. Note: the new diagnostic will read 'not achievable at current data' until a live run produces real resolved trades to populate the reliability curve -- same live-run dependency blocking full validation of everything else this campaign. Source, ex4, and ledger attached.
